roles.348988: triggering alert: medium-integrity explorer launches sdclt.exe, which starts at high integrity
roles.348643: the per-user Folder shell open handler is set to a PowerShell command
roles.348647: DelegateExecute is set to an empty value for the hijacked handler
roles.349079: high-integrity sdclt.exe starts high-integrity control.exe for BackupAndRestoreCenter
roles.349211: high-integrity control.exe starts the configured PowerShell payload at high integrity
roles.350757: the per-user Folder shell open command key is deleted after execution

metadata.case_name: sdclt.exe auto-elevation UAC bypass via Folder shell open command hijack
metadata.security_proposition: The per-user Folder handler hijack caused a PowerShell payload to execute at high integrity through sdclt.exe and control.exe.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = STRONG. The registry hijack and sdclt process chain establish the bypass mechanism and elevated execution. Do not leak to model.

boundary_definition: The case follows one registry-hijack lifecycle from handler creation through elevated payload execution and cleanup.
The later key deletion is retained as mitigating cleanup evidence; it does not undo the observed high-integrity execution.

curation_notes: Subtle-strong (up-rank): the surface is two Microsoft system binaries running (sdclt.exe -> control.exe /name Microsoft.BackupAndRestoreCenter), which reads as benign backup / control-panel activity and invites under-triage. The package does not perform signature verification.

Confirmed malice sits in the EVs: HKCU Classes\Folder\shell\open\command\(Default) is hijacked to PowerShell and DelegateExecute emptied, sdclt auto-elevates (Medium->High), and control.exe starts that PowerShell payload at High integrity. The key is later deleted. Confirmed UAC bypass; correct verdict malicious/high.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
